summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of QTO application. All 27 review test cases verified across login, dashboard, project view, BOQ table (CRUD + inline edit), drawings tab, rate analysis modal, exports (CSV/JSON), header collapse, register/login/logout flow, and new-user project creation. All critical user flows pass.

issues: React hydration warning: <span> rendered inside <option> element via dev tooling instrumentation (x-source attributes wrapper). Causes console error: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Likely benign dev-mode injection from Emergent's source-mapping tool, but should be verified does not appear in production build.

test_results_detail.dashboard_shows_sample_project: PASS - 'Sample Project - Office Building' card visible
test_results_detail.project_header_visible: PASS - PRJ-2026-001 + ACME Construction Ltd shown in metadata panel (confirmed via screenshot; values live in input fields)
test_results_detail.boq_default_tab_seven_rows: PASS - 7 rows with item_nos [1.1.1, 1.2.1, 2.1.1, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 3.1.1, 4.1.1] (NOTE: review request lists '1.1.1 through 4.1.1' and includes 1.1.2; seeded data uses 1.2.1 instead - confirm with main agent if 1.1.2 was intended)
test_results_detail.inline_edit_recalculates: PASS - changing length on row 1.1.1 from 24 to 5 changed qty 648.000 -> 135.000
test_results_detail.rate_analysis_tab: PASS - shows 'Add Rate Analysis' button, modal opens with BOQ selector
test_results_detail.export_csv: PASS - file 'Sample Project - Office Building_boq.csv' downloaded
test_results_detail.export_json: PASS - file 'Sample Project - Office Building_data.json' downloaded
test_results_detail.measure_modal_opens: PASS - DrawingMeasurement modal opens on Ruler icon click
test_results_detail.new_user_register: PASS - registered qauser{ts}@test.com and redirected to dashboard
test_results_detail.create_project_new_user: PASS - project appears in dashboard list after creation
test_results_detail.industrial_dark_theme: PASS - navy/charcoal bg, amber accents observed in screenshots; no white/light backgrounds
test_results_detail.data_testid_attributes: PASS - all interactive elements have data-testid

action_items: (MINOR) Verify seeded item numbers match PRD: review states '1.1.1 through 4.1.1' implying 1.1.2 exists, but seed has 1.2.1 instead of 1.1.2. Confirm with PM if discrepancy is acceptable.

(MINOR) React hydration warning in RateAnalysis CreateRateModal - <span> wrapper injected by dev-tooling inside <option>. Verify the warning does not appear in production build. If issue persists, render plain option text without using template literals that get instrumented by source-map tooling.
critical_code_review_comments: BOQTable.js handleDuplicateRow appends ' (Copy)' to item_no which may break numeric ordering schemes; consider auto-incrementing trailing segment instead.
ProjectView.js fetchProjectData uses Promise.all but if any one of the 3 requests fails, none of the state is populated. Consider independent error handling.
BOQRow's checkbox change for is_deduction calls onUpdate immediately while text fields wait for blur - inconsistent UX, but functionally OK.
BOQTable.handleDeleteRow uses window.confirm() - okay for MVP but a styled modal would match the industrial theme better.
Inline edit comparison uses JSON.stringify equality - works but is fragile if field order changes.
test_credentials: admin@qto.com / admin123 (seeded admin with sample project)
seed_data_creation: Created test user qauser{timestamp}@test.com with password testpass123 + 1 project 'QA Project {timestamp}' during registration flow test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 27 frontend flows in the review request are verified passing. Sample project has 7 BOQ rows (item_nos 1.1.1, 1.2.1, 2.1.1, 2.2.1, 2.2.2 [deduction], 3.1.1, 4.1.1) with TOTAL=1792.956. Inline edit blur-save works. Exports trigger downloads. Register creates user + empty dashboard. Only one observed dev-mode console warning about <span> inside <option> in RateAnalysis modal (likely Emergent dev-tooling instrumentation, not actual app code). No backend or integration issues observed.
